steps: A square with side length $r$ has perimeter $4r$. Two squares contribute $2 \times 4r = 8r$ total.
An equilateral triangle with side length $t$ has perimeter $3t$. Six triangles contribute $6 \times 3t = 18t$ total.

• Choice A — '$6r+24t=210$': Uses 6 and 24 as coefficients; neither matches the correct perimeter totals of 8r and 18t. ✗
  • Choice B — '$2r+6t=210$': Uses the counts of shapes (2 and 6) rather than the perimeters; forgets to multiply by sides per shape. ✗
  • Choice C — '$8r+18t=210$': $8r$ = 2 squares × 4 sides each; $18t$ = 6 triangles × 3 sides each. ✓
  • Choice D — '$6r+2t=210$': Swaps the coefficients entirely; has no relationship to the geometry of the problem. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
